exact.compact_heat_superpolynomial: for every p>0 there exists K_p such that sup_(0<=T<=100) delta_k(T)<=2*k^(-p), k>=K_p
exact.determinant_consequence: the complete-kernel logarithmic ratio correction is o(k^-N) uniformly for every fixed N and every fixed local difference order
exact.local_difference_consequence: for every p>0 and fixed m, sup_(0<=T<=100)|nabla^m e_k(T)|=O_p,m(k^(-p))
exact.m100_superpolynomial: for every p>0 there exists K_p such that 0<=delta_k(100)<=2*k^(-p), k>=K_p

proof_boundary: This artifact proves non-effective superpolynomial higher-theta suppression and all fixed local log-difference bounds uniformly for -100<=lambda<=0. It does not prove the first-summand heat-tilt ratio expansion, uniform order-four positivity, forward order-four invariance, PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda<=0.
remaining_target: prove the first-summand heat-tilt finite-difference estimate uniformly for 0<=T<=100

rows.claim: The inherited low-region probability exponent dominates every fixed logarithmic power target.
rows.proof_boundary: Non-effective asymptotic consequence of the proved lambda=-100 low-region bound.

rows.claim: The inherited pointwise high-region theta-tail bound dominates every fixed inverse power.
rows.proof_boundary: Non-effective asymptotic consequence of the proved high-region bound.

rows.claim: At lambda=-100, the complete-to-first-summand relative moment tail is superpolynomially small.
rows.formula: for every p>0 there exists K_p such that 0<=delta_k(100)<=2*k^(-p), k>=K_p
rows.proof_boundary: All sufficiently large indices for each fixed power; thresholds are not effectivized.

rows.claim: Heat-parameter monotonicity transfers superpolynomial first-summand dominance uniformly to the full compact interval.
rows.formula: for every p>0 there exists K_p such that sup_(0<=T<=100) delta_k(T)<=2*k^(-p), k>=K_p

rows.claim: Every fixed local finite difference of the logarithmic higher-summand correction is uniformly superpolynomial.
rows.formula: for every p>0 and fixed m, sup_(0<=T<=100)|nabla^m e_k(T)|=O_p,m(k^(-p))
rows.proof_boundary: Fixed finite difference order only; sufficient for every fixed determinant size.

rows.claim: Higher theta summands are negligible to every fixed order in the compact-heat order-four ratio expansion.
rows.formula: the complete-kernel logarithmic ratio correction is o(k^-N) uniformly for every fixed N and every fixed local difference order
rows.proof_boundary: Closes only the higher-summand remainder; the first-summand heat-tilt asymptotic remains separate.

source_diagnostics.transfer_status: exact heat-parameter dominance transfer with open lambda-zero curvature tail

status: uniform compact-heat superpolynomial first-summand dominance theorem
